reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone AND on-page in headless Chrome; q8 confirmed non-degenerate across 5 languages; first-visit Download → real classification, zero console errors), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED twice (headless Chrome, WASM, transformers.js 3.7.5). Standalone probe: AutoTokenizer + AutoModelForSequenceClassification('onnx-community/bert-multilingual-toxicity-classifier-ONNX', {dtype:'q8'}) returned a REAL 2-logit head with a clean softmax across languages — en benign pToxic 0.004 / en toxic 0.998; ar benign 0.022 / ar toxic 0.989; es 0.004/0.997; ru toxic 0.998; de benign 0.005. On the built page (fresh profile): Download state → clicking Download reached ready, and the default benign English line scored ALLOW (toxic 0.4%, logits neutral 3.015 / toxic -2.513) with ZERO console errors and ZERO failed network requests.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker (new Worker(worker.js)); the low-level tokenizer→model()→softmax path yields raw logits AND probabilities for the 'see inside' table.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = two logits [neutral(idx0), toxic(idx1)] → softmax summing to 1. Verified exactly that shape (numLabels=2). Semantic sanity confirmed: mild toxic lines score >0.98 toxic, benign lines <0.03, across English/Arabic/Spanish/Russian/German. Label order (0=neutral, 1=toxic) matches the base model card.
rubric.notes: A DISTINCT capability + shape vs the built English toxicity-detection (Xenova/toxic-bert), which is MULTI-LABEL (six independent sigmoids). This is binary single-label softmax, multilingual (15 languages).

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (onnx-community/bert-multilingual-toxicity-classifier-ONNX, linked), the base weights (textdetox/bert-multilingual-toxicity-classifier), the task (text-classification, binary single-label), backend (WASM), quantisation (q8), size (~170 MB) and license (OpenRAIL++). Inference is off the main thread.
rubric.notes: dtype honesty: q8 (model_quantized.onnx, ~170 MB) chosen after confirming it is NON-degenerate; fp32 is 678 MB (too big for browser), fp16 339 MB. q8 gives clean separation, disclosed in at-a-glance.

rubric.evidence: The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (no hand-rolled Load button). Verified on-page: first visit (fresh profile) showed the Download-required state with a 'Download model (~170 MB)' button; clicking it reached state='ready' and auto-ran a score on the default text.
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on the ONNX repo so the ~170 MB cache is tracked for the never-silently-re-download guarantee; a per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/scoring state is announced in role=status aria-live regions; the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path on failure; scoring failures surface on the page as a status .err ('Scoring failed: …'), not only the console. Console + network stayed clean across the first-visit download.
rubric.notes: Practical/multimodel pages show per-item progress ('Scoring comment N of M…', 'Gating comment N…').

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the multilingual textarea (dir=auto so RTL input renders correctly), the language sample chips (benign/toxic per language), the moderation-threshold range slider (accent-color themed, ≥1.6rem), and the Score button. Practical adds a comment-stream textarea + Triage; wild adds a radio group (hostile/friendly) + Score-all; multimodel chains the toxicity gate + a sentiment model with two loaders.
rubric.notes: Threshold slider re-renders the verdict/bars live without re-running inference (cached last score).

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real transformers.js forward pass; verdict, probability bars and the logit/softmax table are all derived from the real 2-logit tensor. The standalone probe confirmed the exact probabilities the UI shows.
rubric.notes: Sober framing throughout: outputs are labelled a probability, not a judgment about a person; the model card's weak-language F1 (down to 0.51 Arabic) is surfaced next to every language.

rubric.evidence: All buttons have accessible names; probability bars use role=meter with aria-valuemin/max/now and an aria-label; status uses role=status aria-live; the range slider has an associated <label>. RTL text (Arabic, Hebrew) is set with dir=rtl / dir=auto and lang attributes so bidi renders correctly. Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system.
rubric.notes: Logical CSS properties (inline-size, margin-inline, text-align:start, border-inline-start) so the layout mirrors correctly in RTL.

rubric.evidence: Verified 0px horizontal overflow at mobile (360×740, DPR3, touch) and clean desktop (1280×800) via scripts/responsive-check. Bars/threshold/samples are max-inline-size bounded and flex-wrap; queue rows flex-wrap with min-inline-size:0 on the text column so long unbreakable strings don't spill; textarea is inline-size:100%.
rubric.notes: support seeded via responsive-check (desktop ok / mobile ok).

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a Web Worker; typing is debounced (350 ms) before re-scoring; the threshold slider re-renders from the cached last score without re-running the model. Single-text latency measured ~86 ms on-page (WASM). No main-thread inference or unbounded loop.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — scoring never blocks the interface.

guidanceConsulted.query: RTL bidirectional Arabic text rendering dir rtl lang mixed content; logical properties
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Prefer logical properties/values (margin-inline-start, text-align:start) so styles adapt to writing modes; ask 'would I want this to flip in RTL?'.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the multilingual textarea uses dir=auto so bidi is inferred per input; Arabic/Hebrew samples set dir=rtl + lang; layout uses inline-size, margin-inline, text-align:start, border-inline-start throughout so it mirrors correctly.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: Arabic sample scored on-page (ar toxic 0.989) with correct RTL rendering; no LTR/RTL clipping at 360×740.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel and queue without horizontal overflow mobile
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + min-inline-size:0 and intrinsic sizing; avoid fixed widths and 100vw.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — queue rows flex-wrap with min-inline-size:0 on the text column; bars/threshold max-inline-size bounded; textarea inline-size:100%.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0px mobile overflow measured at 360×740 in headless Chrome (responsive-check).

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible form controls labels live region status announcements range slider meter
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; label every control; use role=meter with aria-value* for progress-like bars.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + page status are role=status aria-live; probability bars are role=meter with aria-valuenow + aria-label; slider + textarea have <label>s.

guidanceConsulted.query: match range slider and progress bars to site palette without replacing them
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use accent-color to theme native range/checkbox controls instead of custom widgets.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the moderation-threshold range input uses accent-color:var(--accent) and a ≥1.6rem min-block-size tap target.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low, off main thread inference, debounce re-score
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep long-running JS off the main thread and avoid re-running expensive work on every keystroke.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all inference is in a Web Worker; typing is debounced 350 ms; the threshold slider re-renders from a cached score without re-running the model.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: on-page single-text latency ~86 ms; UI responsive; console clean.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.

openQuestions: The model is weakest in Arabic and German (test F1 ~0.51); should the UI show a per-input confidence caveat when the detected language is a low-F1 one?
Would a calibration curve (predicted vs. observed toxicity on a labelled multilingual set) make the 'this is a probability, route low-confidence to humans' message more concrete?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a language-detection step (built language-identification model) so the UI can label the input language and surface that language's reported F1 as a confidence caveat.
On the wild parity page, add a small labelled multilingual set and show a per-language calibration/agreement chart rather than a single hostile/friendly toggle.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for multilingual-toxicity (onnx-community/bert-multilingual-toxicity-classifier-ONNX, text-classification, q8, ~170 MB — an mBERT TextDetox-2025 fine-tune moderating 15 languages with a BINARY toxic/neutral softmax; a DISTINCT capability + shape vs the built English multi-label toxic-bert). RUNNABILITY VERIFIED standalone (real 2-logit head; en 0.004/0.998, ar 0.022/0.989, es/ru/de clean separation) AND on-page (first-visit Download → real ALLOW verdict on benign English, WASM 86 ms, ZERO console/network errors). Ladder: basics (score one line in any language), practical (cross-language moderation queue, worst-first), wild (cross-language parity probe exposing weak languages), multi-model (toxicity gate → multilingual sentiment; only clean text is analysed). Sober framing; per-language F1 (0.51–0.95) surfaced; RTL (Arabic/Hebrew) rendered with dir + logical properties. License OpenRAIL++, recorded in at-a-glance + footer.
